name: vscode-csharp-copy-as-json
description: Right-click a variable in the VS Code Variables view during a C#/.NET debug session and copy it to the clipboard as pretty-printed JSON.

csharpDebugCopyAsJson.allowedDebugTypes.markdownDescription: List of `debugType` values for which the **Copy as JSON** command will run when invoked. The static menu `when` clause currently matches `coreclr` and `clr` only; this setting acts as a runtime guard for additional types you trigger via the command palette or keybinding (e.g. `mono`, `unity`).

csharpDebugCopyAsJson.evaluateTimeoutMs.markdownDescription: Maximum time in milliseconds to wait for the debug adapter to evaluate the serialization expression before aborting. Long property getters or large object graphs can hang the debug UI; the timeout protects against that.

csharpDebugCopyAsJson.preferNewtonsoft.markdownDescription: If `true`, attempt `Newtonsoft.Json.JsonConvert.SerializeObject` first and fall back to `System.Text.Json.JsonSerializer.Serialize`. Useful when the debugged process references Newtonsoft.Json and you rely on its conventions (e.g. `ReferenceLoopHandling`).

csharpDebugCopyAsJson.trace.markdownDescription: When `true`, write timestamped diagnostic logs (expression sent, DAP response shape, fallback decisions) to the **Copy as JSON** output channel. Off by default to avoid persisting potentially sensitive variable values to logs.
